title: galeopsis ladanum subsp. cebennensis
intro: Galeopsis ladanum subsp. cebennensis, commonly known as a subspecies of the Hebrew Lavender or Ladanum, is a perennial herb native to southern Europe. Its medicinal properties include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Galeopsis ladanum subsp. cebennensis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from its leaves and flowers, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
